diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers kept the frozen unsupported label, but they differed in how completely they grounded it. Option 1 cites the full evidence bundle relevant to the claim that a single success can be generalized: single success, no replication, unknown scope, and the current generalization request. That citation set more adequately matches the stated rationale. Option 2 cites only the single-success evidence while its rationale refers to no replication and unknown scope, so its citation set is less adequate relative to its own explanation. On reasons, both include the core idea that the central proposition is unsupported, but option 1 additionally marks insufficient_evidence, which better captures why generalization fails here. Confidence is not meaningfully different because both are high. The overall difference is mixed because it combines an evidence-subset difference with reason-code granularity/completeness differences.
